vision: Distribution framework for Claude Code skills, hooks, and project scaffold templates. Authors maintain deliverables in src/; the build pipeline packages them into release.zip for GitHub Releases install. Success at MVP: an end user can install the toolset into their own project and get a complete, working agentic workflow (orchestrator + planner + router + coding patterns) plus a high-quality agent context (CLAUDE.md family) without manual assembly. Vision derived from repo CLAUDE.md/README by /ts-project:plan --new — correct here if wrong.

releases.name: MVP
releases.goal: End users who run ts-project-init-advisor get the golden agent-context template set (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md, .claude/goverance_CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md) — created directly where absent, recommended as MODIFY tasks where files already exist. Goal derived by --sync; human amends as needed.
exit_criteria: Advisor run in a project with none of the five golden files present creates all five directly from golden templates, byte-identical to src/project_root_structure/ sources
Advisor run in a project with an existing CLAUDE.md emits a MODIFY recommendation task in PROJECT_INIT_PLAN.md and does not touch the existing file
Golden templates ship inside the advisor skill at assets/golden/, populated by a build-time sync from src/project_root_structure/, and the release build fails on parity mismatch between the two
Coexistence posture with install.sh SCAFFOLD=y documented (delegate, deprecate, or bootstrap-only)

releases.name: Iter1
releases.goal: ts-orchestrate becomes a real dual-track orchestrator: Workflow Guidance + Workflow Routing tables cover both Discovery and Delivery; ts-event-storming-facilitator and ts-ddd-tactical-validator ship as sub-agents (closing a gap referenced but unbuilt since the planner PRD); WORK_TYPE expands to the 9-type set (feature/bugfix/hotfix/refactor/chore/patch/spike/poc/ops). Goal derived by --sync from idea-002; human amends as needed.
exit_criteria: npm test fully green, including previously-failing hook tests (.ai→.agents fixture fix)
dist/release.zip contains agents/ts-event-storming-facilitator.md and agents/ts-ddd-tactical-validator.md; npm run dogfood mirrors both into .claude/agents/
ts-orchestrate SKILL.md contains both the Workflow Guidance (Track|Phase/State|Guidance) and Workflow Routing (Work type|Route) tables per tasks/dual-track-orchestration-plan.md §0 D7
/ts-discover explore and /ts-discover decide build enforce the facilitator-required and ddd-validator-required gates (D2/D3)

releases.name: Iter2
releases.goal: inject-workflow-state.sh reads as a direct transcription of ts-orchestrate SKILL.md’s Workflow Guidance table — flat guard-clause structure, one lookup function per track, no nested if/else.
exit_criteria: Hook output byte-identical for every existing test case: all 24 hook tests (hook-output 21 + gate-enforcement 3) pass UNCHANGED — no test edits
Each [NEXT] case row carries a comment or structure that maps 1:1 to a Workflow Guidance table row in ts-orchestrate/SKILL.md
No if/elif nesting deeper than 1 level; guard clauses exit early
npm run pilot PASS (installed-hook smoke)

releases.name: Iter3
releases.goal: Manifest-category installer/build duplication (skills/hooks/agents wired 3x each across build-release.mjs, dogfood.mjs, install.sh, install.ps1) collapses to one reusable pattern per file, with zero change to installed output.
exit_criteria: npm run pilot PASS: local-zip install produces byte-identical installed tree (skills/, hooks incl. scope routing + chmod +x, agents/ with .md suffix) to pre-refactor
node scripts/verify-install.mjs passes (sh/ps1 parity)
npm run release + npm run dogfood produce identical dist/release.zip contents and .claude/ mirror to pre-refactor (diff -r against a captured baseline)
Adding a hypothetical 4th manifest category requires editing exactly one call site per file, not a new copy-pasted loop

releases.name: Iter4
releases.goal: Ship PLDD portable enforcement kernel and inactive consumer adapters without changing current dual-track workflow or activating PLDD in agenticToolset.
exit_criteria: Every implementation file changed by Phase A and Phase B is below src/*; root package configuration, lockfile, live .agents state, hooks, router, and registries remain unchanged
Planted architecture and contract violations produce identical stable rule ids and exit codes through Codex and Claude Code entry paths
Versioned ownership relations preserve legacy exploration arrays and compile deterministic scenario skeletons with complete event coverage
Consumer adapters install only into isolated fixtures, produce a generated-file manifest, and prove rollback; no dogfood activation occurs
EPIC-PLDD-CONSUMER-ADAPTERS starts only after EPIC-PLDD-ENFORCEMENT-KERNEL completes

epics.title: Golden agent-context templates, recommended-to-add by ts-project-init-advisor
epics.notes: Scope v3 (see discovery.json idea-001 notes for pivot history): absent target file → direct create from golden template (absent-only guard, no approval needed); existing target file → gap analysis vs golden emits approval-gated MODIFY task in PROJECT_INIT_PLAN.md, never auto-overwrite. Top M-risk: distinguishing intentional user customization from missing golden content. Golden set = 5 files under src/project_root_structure/: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md, .claude/goverance_CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md (fifth added 2026-07-04 by user decision — copilot-instructions.md counts as agent context). Still excluded from golden set: .claude/settings.json + settings.local.json (machine/permission config, installer-patched), docs/, tasks/, .github/backend.instructions.md + frontend.instructions.md (stack-specific stubs, not universal context). TECHNICAL REQUIREMENT (2026-07-04): authoring stays in src/project_root_structure/; delivery mechanism = golden templates ship INSIDE the advisor skill package at assets/golden/, populated by a build-time sync step in the release pipeline, guarded by a parity check that fails the build when assets/golden/ differs from src/project_root_structure/. Rationale: advisor installs to .claude/skills/ on every user machine (no SCAFFOLD=y dependency, no installer re-run needed, no network fetch); assets/ is the agent-skills-spec directory for templates so skill-validator accepts it natively; dogfood mirror inherits it via the existing sync since assets/ is not exclusion-filtered. Rejected: installer copies to a well-known path (breaks for skill-only updates); runtime GitHub fetch (network dependency + version skew).

epics.title: Dual-track orchestration: guidance/routing tables, Discovery sub-agents (ts-event-storming-facilitator, ts-ddd-tactical-validator), WORK_TYPE expansion
epics.notes: Execution runbook: tasks/dual-track-orchestration-plan.md (T1-T12, weak-model-executable, 12 locked decisions in §0 — do not re-open in Think/Plan). Single epic despite 4 bounded_contexts (discovery/delivery/packaging/orchestration): the runbook's T1-T12 sequence and single Spectra proposal (T11) assume they ship together; splitting would break the dependency order. Carried forward from idea-002 validate (non-blocking, address during Plan/Build): (1) add a JSON-shape/type check for sub-agent output on top of the existing non-empty gate; (2) add a test asserting phase-routing.ts spines match SKILL.md's Workflow Routing table and work-unit-profiles.md; (3) DDD model gap — Release/Iteration under-modeled as its own aggregate, WorkflowStateInjected mis-classified as a domain event rather than a projection (informational, not a code fix). Known pre-existing test breakage fixed in passing (T8): hook-output.test.ts / gate-enforcement.test.ts fixtures use .ai/ but hook reads .agents/ — declare in Think, not silent scope creep.

epics.title: Restructure inject-workflow-state.sh to mirror the Workflow Guidance table
epics.notes: User-reported readability problem 2026-07-05: algorithm hard to read, if-else complicated. Target: script structure follows SKILL.md Workflow Guidance table — guard-clause preamble (schema/dry-run/G2 checks as named functions), then one flat case block per track whose rows visibly correspond to table rows. Behavior change: NONE — existing 24 hook tests are the regression harness and must pass unedited. No Discovery needed (refactor of shipped code, no new capability).

epics.title: Deduplicate manifest-category loops across build-release.mjs, dogfood.mjs, install.sh, install.ps1
epics.notes: Tracked open item since EPIC-DUAL-TRACK-ORCHESTRATION Review phase (docs/Ideas.md §12/§14): skills/hooks/agents manifest categories each wired as a copy-pasted per-category loop in 4 files. Higher risk than the hook refactor (Iter2) -- this is installer code that runs on end-user machines via curl|bash / irm|iex, not a local dev hook. Full spine with exhaustive pilot/verify-install/release/dogfood verification before Ship.

epics.title: PLDD portable enforcement kernel
epics.notes: Phase A only. All implementation changes below src/*. Zero new npm dependencies. Deliver fail-closed architecture/placement checking, stable diagnostics, contract-id parity, versioned ownership relations, scenario compilation, and fixture tests. Current dual-track configuration remains unchanged.

epics.title: PLDD inactive consumer-project adapters
epics.notes: Phase B only; depends on Phase A. Add inactive scaffold/install adapters, optional dependency-cruiser/ESLint generation, cards, agent contracts, templates, Build checklist, fixture-only dry-run, generated-file manifest, and rollback plan. All implementation changes below src/*. No live agenticToolset activation. Dogfood requires separate approval.

constraints: TypeScript 5.x strict ESM, Node runtime, Vitest
Skills must be self-contained — no cross-skill imports
Security gates G1/G2 always require human sign-off
Packaging goes through scripts/generate-manifest.mjs + build-release.mjs with shared exclusion filter
